summary: Add production core commit receipts and explicit runtime side-effect policy.
rationale: This slice projects a path-free production commit receipt from RuntimeFinalizationRevision and separates prod side-effect behavior from dev artifact and learning-state writes.
verification: uv run pytest tests/test_runtime_core_commit.py tests/test_runtime_side_effects.py tests/test_runtime_production_contract.py tests/test_api_production_contract.py -q

uv run ruff check src/seektalent/runtime/core_commit.py src/seektalent/runtime/side_effects.py src/seektalent/runtime/production_contract.py src/seektalent/api.py tests/test_runtime_core_commit.py tests/test_runtime_side_effects.py
uv run ty check src tests tools
uv run pytest tests/test_liepin_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_opencli_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_cli.py tests/test_liepin_boundaries.py -q
deletion_targets: production result contracts that omit the runtime finalization commit receipt
core commit receipts that expose local filesystem paths or debug artifact locations
implicit runtime side-effect behavior that treats prod and dev writes the same
risks: The core commit receipt is intentionally derived from RuntimeFinalizationRevision; later persistence slices must keep the same idempotency key shape when writing to durable stores.
The side-effect policy is a production-facing decision surface only; later runtime wiring must use it without reintroducing debug or flywheel writes in prod.
